inputs.label: Incoming Emails
inputs.description: Emails from consultants, contractors, and project authorities — in varied formats with mixed attachment types — arrive in the shared project inbox.
inputs.inputFormat: Free-text emails with mixed attachments — PDFs, CAD files, and scanned documents.

persona.quote: I get dozens of emails a week from different consultants — tracking which ones need a ticket and making sure attachments are filed correctly takes half my day.

stations.label: CAPTURE
stations.description: Ingests each email and confirms it belongs to an active project.

agents.name: Email Review
agents.description: Opens each incoming email, confirms the sender is a known project contact, and turns it into structured project context.
agents.output: Verified email, matched to the correct project.

stations.label: UNDERSTAND
stations.description: Classifies the content, extracts attachment metadata, and retrieves the project's ISO 19650 taxonomy.

agents.name: Content Extraction
agents.description: Reads every attachment — PDFs, CAD files, scanned documents — and pulls out the text and data needed for classification.
agents.output: Full text and data extracted from all attachments.

agents.name: SGBuildex
agents.description: Queries the SGBuildex API to retrieve the official HDB naming convention and folder structure for this project, ensuring ISO 19650 compliance.
agents.output: Naming convention, folder structure, and required fields — ready to apply.

agents.name: Classification
agents.description: Tags each document with its type, engineering discipline, and site location — using the ISO 19650 taxonomy.
agents.output: Document tagged: type, discipline, location, and more.

stations.label: STRUCTURE
stations.description: Creates a tracked issue in Autodesk and files attachments to the ISO 19650 standard.

agents.name: Ticketing
agents.description: Raises a formal issue in Autodesk — setting the type, assignee, and description so it flows straight into the project's tracking workflow.
agents.output: An Autodesk Issue — typed, assigned, and linked to the project.

agents.name: Document Archival
agents.description: Renames each attachment for ISO 19650 compliance, moves it to the correct folder, and fills in all required metadata fields.
agents.output: Attachments filed: compliant naming, correct folder, full metadata.

stations.label: GOVERN
stations.description: Archives the email thread as a traceable decision record in the project's Common Data Environment.

agents.name: Decision Record
agents.description: Saves the full email thread to SharePoint as an official decision record — capturing who decided what, when, and against which documents.
agents.output: Decision record in SharePoint — traceable and searchable by Copilot.

outputs.label: Automated Ticketing
outputs.description: Every actionable email becomes a tracked Autodesk issue — assigned, typed, and project-linked automatically.

outputs.label: Compliant Archiving
outputs.description: Attachments filed in the right folder with compliant names and full metadata — no manual renaming.

outputs.label: Decision Traceability
outputs.description: Every decision has a paper trail — stored in SharePoint and searchable by Copilot.

steps.caption: An HDB project officer tracks dozens of emails a week — manual triage, renaming, and filing consume half their day.
Emails arrive from multiple stakeholders — varied formats, attachment types, and levels of detail.
The Email Review Agent ingests each email and confirms project context.
Classification, Document Analysis, and SGBuildex Agents tag the content and retrieve the ISO 19650 taxonomy.
The Ticketing Agent raises an Autodesk Issue; the Document Archival Agent files attachments to spec.
The Decision Record Agent archives the email thread to SharePoint as an ISO 19650 decision record.
A typed Autodesk Issue is created and linked to the project.
Attachments are filed under ISO 19650-compliant names with full metadata.
The email thread is preserved as a decision record — Copilot can now trace who decided what and why.
